summary: Tests/contracts still preserve project-local .fabric/knowledge dual-root migration, doctor fallback synthesis, watcher invalidation, and workspace-relative pending path contracts despite store-only intent.

Runtime hooks and doctor apply-lint still resolve pending/canonical knowledge through project-local .fabric/knowledge, ~/.fabric/knowledge, or .fabric/agents.meta.json instead of mounted store read/write-target resolution.

User-facing docs and packaged skill templates still tell developers/agents to author, glob, review, or display project-local .fabric/knowledge and ~/.fabric/knowledge paths even though knowledge should live only in mounted stores.
User-facing docs, AI skill templates, and hook templates still mention or scan project-local .fabric/knowledge / .fabric/agents.meta.json despite store-only mounted-store architecture.

Legacy migration/fallback surfaces still expose or retain dual-root knowledge behavior: store migrate scans .fabric/knowledge and ~/.fabric/knowledge, experimental HTTP watches local knowledge globs, and doctor/i18n keeps filesystem-edit fallback contracts.

fabric onboard-coverage's implementation resolves mounted store read-sets, but its tests seed project-local .fabric/knowledge fixtures and only call runOnboardCoverage directly, leaving the public mounted-store onboard workflow untested.
HooksStage converts step exceptions into InstallStepResult status=error but still returns stageRan('hooks'), and tests do not cover a failing hook/config/bootstrap step.
PreflightStage claims to validate target/global-root writability and git availability, but assertWritable and assertGitAvailable are empty and no test references those negative paths.

Executable dogfood harnesses still call extractKnowledge with legacy source_session and review local .fabric/knowledge paths even though current MCP/schema/service contracts require source_sessions[] and store write-target paths.

HTTP integration header claims MCP initialize handshake coverage, but the only successful initialize/session-creation assertion is an unconditional it.skip; existing 400/404 tests exercise failure branches only.
CLI and server coverage gates are configured below the project 80% target while root test:coverage runs those package gates, reducing the red signal expected from coverage policy.
The CLI i18n install snapshot pins Cursor capability output as hook/skill n/a even though install writes Cursor hooks/bootstrap and the parity matrix declares Cursor full hook/skill/mcp support.
